goal: Find which Allegro hand link has zero mass causing intermittent PxScene::simulate crashes at env reset, without grep-diving 23 URDF links.
thoughts: PxScene zero-mass usually means a rigid-body link inherited no PhysicsMassAPI, or unit scale mismatch made 0.05kg read as ~0 after metersPerUnit scaling. Push back against the blanket 'mass=1.0' fix — it destroys inertia for contact-rich manipulation. Enumerate links programmatically: zero-volume collision or missing MassAPI are the two real culprits.
code:
# 1. Structured diagnosis on the captured PhysX error
diagnose_physics_error(error_text="PxScene::simulate(): Actor has zero mass")

# 2. Scope a stage analysis to the articulation
run_stage_analysis(scope="<ROBOT_PRIM_PATH>")  # e.g. /World/envs/env_0/Robot

# 3. Enumerate rigid bodies and dump mass/density — zeros surface immediately
list_all_prims(root="<ROBOT_PRIM_PATH>", schema_filter="PhysicsRigidBodyAPI")
run_usd_script(script="""
from pxr import Usd, UsdPhysics
stage = omni.usd.get_context().get_stage()
for p in stage.Traverse():
    if p.HasAPI(UsdPhysics.RigidBodyAPI):
        mass_api = UsdPhysics.MassAPI(p) if p.HasAPI(UsdPhysics.MassAPI) else None
        mass = mass_api.GetMassAttr().Get() if mass_api else None
        density = mass_api.GetDensityAttr().Get() if mass_api else None
        if not mass or mass == 0:
            print('ZERO-MASS', p.GetPath(), 'density=', density, 'hasMassAPI=', mass_api is not None)
""")

# 4. metersPerUnit pitfall — resolve authored mass under stage scale
lookup_knowledge(query="USD metersPerUnit URDF mass scale PhysicsMassAPI Isaac Sim 5")
failure_modes: Blanket mass=1.0 hides the bug but destroys contact dynamics for manipulation
URDF importer in 5.x still drops mass when <inertial> is absent — confirm per link, do not guess
metersPerUnit=0.01 with URDF authored in meters silently scales mass, reads as ~0
Zero-volume collision mesh also triggers zero mass under density-based path
